concept.name: Separability-failure predictor v2 (pythia-410m, ~250 diverse configs)
concept.definition: Better-powered rerun. FULL spec + method + prior-run numbers are in research_cycle/queued/separability_boundary_v2.json -- the tester MUST read that file first. Short version: pythia-410m mid layer; 200-300 diverse concept swaps (animal class, size, material, country->continent, profession -- NOT only fruit->veg); per config sweep steering alpha; outcome=clean-window width (FLIP & PRESERVE & COHERENT). Predictors WITHOUT sweep: Park baseline=|cos(swap-dir,attr-dir)| causal-inner-product; OURS=grad-overlap |cos(grad category-margin, grad attr-margin)|+probe-interference. Fit on train configs, test on disjoint.
concept.prediction: Held-out: best internal predictor |Spearman|>=0.5 AND beats Park by >=0.15 (bootstrap CI excludes 0) AND clears random-permutation floor. REFUTED-null below floor; REFUTED-as-rediscovery if nothing beats Park. Oracle steering positive-control must pass else BROKEN_MEASUREMENT. Prior run: ours |rho|=0.32 right sign but <0.36 floor at n=32/gpt2; test if 410m+~250 configs lifts past floor.
concept.reduces_to: PRIOR ART GIVEN, do not re-flag: Park 2024 (arXiv 2406.01506), LRH causal separability, RAVEL (2402.17700), ActAdd (2308.10248). Keep ONLY by mapping FAILURE boundary AND beating orthogonality baseline above chance.

test.design: FAITHFUL v2 (pythia-410m + ~250 diverse configs) is NOT computable in tight budget: 410m is not cached (heavy download) and a 250-config alpha-sweep with per-config gradients is multi-hour on CPU. So I designed and ran a DECISIVE CHEAP PROXY on gpt2 (layer 8, cached, matches prior sb1 for direct comparison) targeting the ONE load-bearing empirical assumption of v2: that adding configs AND swap-family diversity makes the OURS grad-overlap predictor lift past the random-permutation floor. Reused the validated category_slot steering machinery; two families (fruit->vegetable + bird->mammal) to inject diversity; outcome = clean-window width (count of alpha in {1..5} with cat-FLIP AND attr-PRESERVE AND coherence in [0.8,1.2]); predictors computed WITHOUT the sweep: Park = whitened |cos| of swap-dir vs attr-dir in unembedding (causal inner product); OURS = |cos| of grad(cat-margin) vs grad(attr-margin) wrt L8 resid. PREREGISTERED RULE (frozen in the script header before running): BROKEN if oracle<1.0; PROXY_SUPPORTS_V2 if |rho_OURS|>=floor AND >|rho_Park| AND >=0.30 AND sign negative; PROXY_REFUTES if below floor OR sign flips OR <0.15. Explicitly noted the proxy can only de-risk/kill the direction, not confirm the full |rho|>=0.5 v2 bar.
test.numbers: n=25 configs survived token-filtering (21 fruit + only 4 bird; below the 32 of prior sb1 — the diversity injection largely failed). POOLED: rho_OURS=-0.390, rho_Park=-0.213, perm-floor95=0.397, delta_abs=+0.187 but bootstrap 95% CI=[-0.139,+0.412] INCLUDES 0. Mechanical prereg rule -> PROXY_SUPPORTS_V2. BUT the sanity decomposition overturns it: FRUIT-ONLY rho_OURS=-0.127 (COLLAPSED below its own floor 0.436, and weaker than prior sb1's -0.324); the 4 bird configs form a NON-OVERLAPPING cluster (ours 0.268-0.303 vs fruit 0.005-0.134, all bird windows=0). The pooled "lift" is a between-family scale offset (Simpson's paradox), not within-family predictive power. Park pooled sign is the same artifact (fruit-only Park=+0.157, still wrong sign, matching prior). Even the artifact-inflated pooled result misses the full v2 bar: |rho|=0.40<0.50 and Park-margin CI includes 0.
test.sanity: Oracle POSITIVE CONTROL PASSED: adding the Cprime steering direction to GENUINE Cprime members intensified the target category by +1.73 logit (min_oracle=1.0) -> the steering/logit-diff pipeline works, guards the .norm-bug class; near-zero would have meant BROKEN not refuted. Random control: gaussian vector of equal norm flipped 0/4 entities -> effect is direction-specific. Predictor values non-constant/entity-specific (ours 0.005-0.303). CRUCIAL extra sanity (the family decomposition above) revealed the pooled correlation is a cross-family offset artifact, so I do NOT trust the mechanical pooled verdict.
